title: centaurea antennata
intro: Centaurea antennata, also known as White Cockle or Mountain Cockle, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant's medicinal properties and health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular remedy for various health issues. Centaurea antennata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
